mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals an impending economic contraction, prompting equity investors to de-risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, validating corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns in over 60% of observed instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher short-term equity returns during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns during monetary easing periods, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or null correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 235.0, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL breaches 235.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation with a Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly declines in real GDP and monthly increases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized economic downturn, forcing equity markets to price in severe earnings recession and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The co-occurrence of falling GDPC1 and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns in over 65% of historical instances, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant with a p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production crossing above the 2017 baseline of 100.0 signals robust manufacturing output that supports short-term equity momentum, but this relationship only holds when the economy is not in a liquidity crunch.
failure_condition: INDPRO values above 100.0 fail to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction in out-of-sample testing with a negative Rank IC.
